Each stage corresponds to one abstract method on RobotAdaptor or ModelAdaptor, so swapping any one stage in the canvas is symmetric to swapping its underlying class. New robot ⇒ new RobotAdaptor (stages 1+4); new model ⇒ new (ModelAdaptor + BasePolicy) pair (stages 2+3 + GPU). Canvas-level abstraction mirrors the framework abstraction (vlaworkspace's 4 atomic methods). No fused boundaries — the canvas does not hide an intermediate that exists at the Python level. Heavy GPU load happens in predict ; the four adapter nodes are pure-CPU dict shape transforms, fast to re-run when only config changes. 1.1 Where it fits Upstream (obs) RobotAdaptor Downstream (action) env_libero__reset / env_libero__step LiberoRobot (delta or absolute variant) env_libero__step env_simpler__reset / env_simpler__step SimplerRobot (WidowX + Google Robot) env_simpler__step Future: env_franka , env_droid , … new RobotAdaptor subclass under adapters/robots/ same The adapter nodes are pure functions of their inputs + config; they don't talk to the env directly. Coupling with the env is via wire shape (obs ports), not API. 2. Architecture: the Adaptor system The framework is a 4-stage translation pipeline mediated by the CanonicalDict lingua franca. Each stage is one abstract method, packed into one of two adapter classes: Stage Direction Owner class Method 1 env_obs → canonical RobotAdaptor env_to_canonical(env_obs) 2 canonical → model_batch ModelAdaptor canonical_to_model(canonical) 3 model_output → action_canonical ModelAdaptor model_to_canonical(model_output, info) 4 action_canonical → env_action RobotAdaptor canonical_to_env(canonical_action, state) RobotAdaptor × ModelAdaptor → Adaptor (composed) + BasePolicy (stages 1+4) (stages 2+3) (bridge per pair) (per model — stage 3's nn.Module) Any robot × any model is a valid pair. RobotAdaptor knows nothing about which model it'll be fed into; ModelAdaptor knows nothing about which robot produced its inputs. They communicate only through CanonicalDict + CanonicalInfo . The composed Adaptor exposes both single-stage methods ( output_transforms = stage 3 then stage 4 ) and pre-fused convenience methods ( env_input_transforms = stages 1+2 ) — but the canvas calls each atomic stage individually via dedicated manager methods ( model_to_canonical_action , canonical_action_to_env ), keeping each canvas node 1:1 with one abstract method. Source: all four method signatures, CanonicalDict , CanonicalInfo , and the composed Adaptor are vendored verbatim from vlaworkspace/src/vlaworkspace/adaptors/ . AgentCanvas does not import vlaworkspace at runtime — it owns a snapshot. 2.1 CanonicalDict — the lingua franca Every robot maps to this format; every model maps from it. Defined in adapters/canonical.py : CanonicalDict = { "data" : { "images" : { "front" : ndarray | None , "wrist" : ndarray | None }, "state" : { "pos" : … , "rot" : … , "gripper" : … , "joint_position" : … }, # all optional "actions" : { "pos" : … , "rot" : … , "gripper" : … , "joint_position" : … }, # output side "prompt" : str , }, "info" : CanonicalInfo ( state_type , state_rot_repr , action_type , action_rot_repr , state_dims , action_dims ), } The info block is metadata that lets a ModelAdaptor know what kind of rotation (axis-angle vs quaternion vs euler), what keys are populated , etc., so it can normalise correctly without having to know which robot it's paired with. 2.2 RobotAdaptor (abstract) adapters/base_robot.py . Translates between an env's native obs/action format and CanonicalDict . Abstract methods: get_canonical_info () → CanonicalInfo dataset_to_canonical ( data ) → CanonicalDict # for training env_to_canonical ( env_obs ) → CanonicalDict # for inference (no actions) canonical_to_env ( canonical_action , state = None ) → dict # action chunk for env get_state_dim () / get_action_dim () / get_norm_stats_keys () / env_obs () / env_action () / datasets () Currently shipped subclass : LiberoRobot only. 2.3 ModelAdaptor (abstract) adapters/base_model.py . Translates between CanonicalDict and model-specific input/output dicts, and owns normalisation ( norm_stats , loaded from a JSON file at construction). Abstract methods: canonical_to_model ( canonical ) → dict # produces the batch fed into BasePolicy model_to_canonical ( model_output , info ) → CanonicalDict canonical_to_norm_stats_format ( canonical ) → dict # for fitting/loading norm stats get_norm_stats_mode () → str get_norm_stats_keys () → tuple [ str , ... ] model_input () / model_output () → dict # shape descriptors The base class handles norm_stats_path JSON loading uniformly ( mean/std/q01/q99/min/max per key), so subclasses just declare which keys they normalise and how. Currently shipped subclasses : Pi0Model , SmolVLAModel , DPModel (used by both dp and droid_dp ). 2.4 Composed Adaptor…

title: SmartWay on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + smartway port
text: SmartWay on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + smartway port SmartWay (25.03; Shi et al., IROS 2025) is a zero-shot VLN-CE agent: a TRM waypoint predictor proposes candidate (angle, distance) moves from the panorama, RAM+ object tags enrich each candidate's description, and a backtracking return option lets the LLM undo a mistaken move. This page documents the decomposed port — the 7-node smartway reasoning nodeset plus two server-mode siblings ( smartway_waypoint , smartway_perception ) driving env_habitat continuous navigation in smartway_ce.json . The validated 3-node monolith ( smartway_mono ) remains the equivalence ground truth. At a glance Upstream SmartWay (25.03), IROS 2025 — repo sxyxs/SmartWay-Code pinned @ daa2dd8 (MIT). Adelaide Qi Wu group paper — this is a side-experiment port, deliberately excluded from PortBench v1 (author-relationship constraint). Env consumed env_habitat — action space hightolow ( step_hightolow ; (0,0) = canonical STOP), obs space panorama (n_views=12), reset / evaluate ( gym-like contract ) FM consumed built-in llmCall — reference graph pins gpt-5-mini · temp 1.0 · max_tokens 2000 · response_format=json_object (frozen by the AAS constraints file; upstream pinned gpt-4o) Method nodesets smartway (7 local reasoning nodes) + smartway_waypoint__predict (server: vendored TRM predictor + DINOv2-small) + smartway_perception__tag (server: RAM+ swin-L @ 384) — both servers share the ac-smartway conda env ( SMARTWAY_PYTHON override) State 9 lastWrite graph_state entries — 7 episode lifetime + 2 step -lifetime scratch ( last_picked_* ); single-writer-per-key after the decomposition Graph workspace/graphs/vln/verified/smartway_ce.json (21 nodes, 40 edges); sibling monolith smartway_mono_ce.json (still unverified/ , equivalence ground truth, test_equivalence.py ) Fidelity faithful with justified deviations (audited 2026-06-11, inventory-first): task_description byte-identical; stop/parse/latch semantics source-checked; decomposition ↔ monolith equivalence tests 10/10 passed (re-run green 2026-06-12 post-reorg — §3E); bucket E empty after probes (§3E). Status Verified. Locked gpt-5-mini baseline SR 0.29 (run 20260526_163540 , perf rand100, 100 ep). Post-migration re-eval 2026-06-13 (run 20260613_115915 , gpt-5-mini, rand100 20 ep, step_budget 15): SR 0.20 · SPL 0.199 · nDTW 0.431 — non-zero harvest confirms the TODO #64 fix ( 7095f196 ); within 20-ep variance of the 100-ep lock. Graph promoted to graphs/vln/verified/ (§4). 1. Upstream method analysis (Shi et al. 2025) Upstream SmartWay is a VLN-CE rollout ( vlnce_baselines/common/base_il_trainer.py ) around the same OneStagePromptManager class name MapGPT uses ( vlnce_baselines/GPT/one_stage_prompt_manager.py ) — but rewritten for continuous environments with waypoints, tags, and backtracking. Per step: Predict waypoints. The panorama feeds a trained TRM waypoint predictor ( waypoint_predictor/TRM_net.py , BinaryDistPredictor_TRM + DINOv2-small features per Policy_ViewSelection_VLNBERT.py:111 ) → K candidate (angle, distance) pairs, each with its view RGB. Tag candidates. RAM+ ( ram_plus , swin-L, image_size 384 — base_il_trainer.py:368–371 ) runs inference_ram on each candidate image ( :440–442 ) → per-candidate object-tag strings. Topology bookkeeping + synthetic return ( make_action_prompt_backtrackv2 , one_stage_prompt_manager.py:44– ): the current position and each candidate are appended to nodes_list ; the index in that list is the episode-global Place ID . last_distance is derived from the previous step's env action ( :54–56 ); when the last step was not itself a backtrack and last_distance ≠ 0 , a synthetic return candidate (angle π, distance = last_distance) is appended ( :81–90 ). Phrase each candidate ( :108–112 ): "<direction> to Place N which is corresponding to Image N, and this image contains objects such as <tags>." — direction from six 60° buckets ( get_action_concept_backtrack , :23–41 ). The trailing return candidate gets the verbatim phrase “Move back to last position in an opposite direction” ( :110 ). Letter options + stop gate ( make_action_options_backtrack , :148–165 ): from t ≥ 2 (hardcoded) a bare stop is prepended; everything is letter-prefixed A/B/C…. Prompts ( make_r2r_json_prompts , :202–245 ): a static 8-component task_description system prompt — including a dedicated Return Policy paragraph — plus a 4-field user prompt ( Instruction / History / Previous Planning / Action options ). gpt_infer_back_track ( api.py:48–95 ) packs candidate images with "Image N:" labels before each image. Parse ( parse_json_action , :294–309 ): letter → index, else 0; at t ≥ 2 the index is decremented to absorb the prepended stop, so −1 means STOP . parse_json_planning ( :284–291 ) extracts New Planning (fallback "No plans currently." ) and appends it to the planning list. Act ( make_equiv_action , base_il_trainer.py:167–204 ): index → the candidate's (angle, distance), executed as rotate + repeated 0.25 m forward primitives. History + backtrack latch ( make_history , :168–184 ): appends "step t: <phrase>" ; if the executed phrase string-equals the return phrase, self.backtrack = True ( :183–184 ) — which suppresses the synthetic return next step. habitat — R2R-CE rollout episode instruction RGBD panorama ×12 instruction TRM predictor TRM_net + DINOv2 candidates topo + return backtrackv2 :44–120 action options stop gate t≥2 · :148–165 RAM+ tags inference_ram :440–442 tags image pack api.py:48–95 prompt assembly make_r2r_json_prompts candidate RGBs topology state options system + user "Image N:" + images GPT-4o call JSON 3-field reply manager attrs history · planning · backtrack nodes_list · graph · trajectory last_distance (:54–56) Python object state, in-process make_history latch :183–184 parse_json_* −1 = STOP · :294–309 make_equiv_action trainer :167–204 chosen phrase index episode end stop or step budget index = −1 (stop) habitat env step rotate + 0.25 m forwards angle · distance R2R-CE eval SR · SPL · nDTW metrics for t in range(max_steps) — next step re-observes simulator side manager / model fn GPT call rollout guard object-attr state data flow attr read/write rollout loop (next t) 1.1 Upstream invariants worth marking Place IDs are episode-global, but there is no map text. Unlike MapGPT, the prompt has no Trajectory / Map / Supplementary fields — nodes_list / graph / trajectory bookkeeping exists to mint stable Place IDs so history phrases written at step t still mean something at step t+k. The backtrack latch is an exact string match. make_history compares the executed phrase against “Move back to last position in an opposite direction” character-for-character ( :183–184 ). Rewording the return phrase silently kills backtracking. The return option is self-suppressing. It is offered only when the previous step was not a backtrack and last_distance ≠ 0 — the agent cannot oscillate by chaining returns. Stop is gated at t ≥ 2 (hardcoded) and −1-encoded. Parse failure decodes as index 0, which after the shift means STOP late and first-candidate early. The system prompt is static. All episode state rides the 4-field user prompt; task_description (with the Return Policy paragraph) never changes across steps. 2. The AgentCanvas port The port is a Phase-2 decomposition of the validated smartway_mono monolith (3 nodes), split along natural seams: plan_step → update_topology / build_action_options / assemble_prompt / build_images ; decide_action → parse_response / resolve_action ; update_history unchanged. Verbatim prompt constants and pure helpers live in smartway_mono/_prompts.py and are imported by both variants; test_equivalence.py checks the decomposition against the monolith. After the split, every graph_state key has exactly one writer. 2.1 Per-step flow env_habitat reset → instruction observe_panorama ×12 instruction (iterIn init) waypoint_predict server · TRM + DINOv2 candidates update_topology mints Place IDs · owns topo build_action_options phrases + letters…

title: Three-Step Nav on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + threestep port
text: Three-Step Nav on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + threestep port Three-Step Nav ( A Hierarchical Global-Local Planner for Zero-Shot Vision-and-Language Navigation , arXiv 2604.26946) is a zero-shot LLM agent for continuous VLN (R2R-CE / RxR-CE) that descends directly from Open-Nav — its agent class literally is class Open_Nav . It runs three steps per episode: (1) global — decompose the instruction into sub-instructions + per-sub landmarks; (2) local — a MapGPT-style image navigator picks the next direction and self-reports Completion Estimation: Yes/No ; (3) verify — when completion is "Yes", a continue / stay / backtrack / look-around judge advances a sub-instruction pointer. The threestep nodeset ports the method-side reasoning ; it reuses Open-Nav's env_habitat + opennav_waypoint (TRM) + opennav_perception (RAM Swin-L + SpatialBot-3B) substrate wholesale. Upstream paper Three-Step Nav (arXiv 2604.26946) — claims zero-shot SOTA on R2R-CE / RxR-CE val_unseen Pinned commit github.com/ZoeyZheng0/3-step-Nav @ 5cdbdcf Env spaces consumed env_habitat — reset · observe_panorama (RGB-D ×12) · step_hightolow (angle, distance) · evaluate FM nodes consumed opennav_waypoint (TRM 12-dir waypoints) · opennav_perception (RAM tags + SpatialBot-3B depth captions) · 4× llmCall Nodes 13 method nodes (mono) · 32-node graph Graph(s) workspace/graphs/vln/unverified/threestep_ce.json (mono) · threestep2_ce.json (decomp) Status vs paper unverified — faithful-config re-eval (gpt-5-mini, 10-ep rand100 ): TL 8.10 ≈ paper 9.18 (trajectory shape reproduced) but SR 0/10 vs paper 0.34 ; the SR gap is model-tier (gpt-5-mini vs paper gpt-5-full), not a port gap (see §4). Earlier gpt-5 50-ep slice: SR 0.16 (pre-grill config) Fidelity verdict faithful with justified deviations (code-fidelity; inventory-first audit + grill-implement iter-1/iter-2, 2026-06-22). All prompts incl. the judge byte-verified, control flow mirrored, Phase-2 decomp byte-equivalent (13/13). Grill iter-2 closed every remaining port-side E item (navigator image detail="high" , judge image-descriptions block, the full stuck-direction subsystem); iter-3 corrected the last two bucket-D premises against the real paper run config — abilities ["continue","stay"] and horizon 8 are now matched, so no 🟠 port-fidelity divergence remains . The only non-faithful axis left is the model tier (gpt-5-mini vs paper gpt-5, bucket C — a standing-directive cost choice); the residual entries (navigator retry/random-fallback, caption-500, the cosmetic dict_keys id format) are substrate/shared-infra/cosmetic, not port defects. The faithful-config re-eval has landed (run 20260622_232311 , gpt-5-mini): TL 8.10 ≈ paper 9.18 confirms the behaviour and SR 0/10 is model-tier; §4 carries the numbers. The graph stays under unverified/ until a gpt-5-full run reproduces the paper SR. 1. Upstream method analysis (Zheng et al. 2026) The driver is base_il_trainer_llm.BaseVLNCETrainerLLM._eval_llm . The agent ( spatialNavigator.Open_Nav ) and judge ( decision_agent ) are pure-Python over an OpenAI client ( api.llmClient ) and the perception stack ( api.spatialClient — RAM + SpatialBot-3B). Per episode: Pre-loop (Step 1, global) — navigator.detect_actions ( ACTION_DETECTION ) splits the instruction into action_list ; navigator.detect_landmarks ( LANDMARK_DETECTION , one call per sub-instruction) builds landmark_list . base_il_trainer_llm.py:589-594 Per step (Step 2, local) — the policy net predicts candidate waypoints ( BinaryDistPredictor_TRM ); construct_image_dicts bins them into the 12 directions (30° each); navigator.observe_environment fuses RAM tags + SpatialBot captions per candidate; navigator.move_to_next_vp_single ( MAPGPT_NAVIGATOR , image prompt over candidate tiles) returns Prediction (next vp) + Completion Estimation . :607-790 Per step (Step 3, verify) — gated on completion_estimation == "Yes" : decision_agent.make_informed_decision_with_capture returns continue / stay / backtrack / look-around. continue advances current_action_idx (resetting per-sub history); at the last sub-instruction continue → stop_flag ; should_stop_navigation also stops on continue / stay at the last sub-instruction. backtrack reverses the last move. :760-839 Env action — {'action': 4, 'action_args': {'angle': radius_dict[next_vp], 'distance': distance_dict[next_vp]}} (HIGHTOLOW). Loop ends at current_step == step_length ( step_length = MAX_EPISODE_STEPS ). :849-875 Step 1 — global (pre-loop) detect_actions · detect_landmarks action_list · landmark_list Step 2 — local (per step) policy.net waypoint (TRM) construct_image_dicts (12-dir) observe_environment (RAM+SpatialBot) move_to_next_vp_single MapGPT image navigator → pred_vp · completion Completion? Yes? gate make_informed_decision continue/stay/backtrack/ look-around manager object attributes current_action_idx · nav_history chosen_images · env_actions_history decision drives the pointer / history envs.step — HIGHTOLOW action 4 (angle, distance) next obs stop_flag Yes @ last sub-instr eval metrics SR · SPL · NE · nDTW for current_step in range(MAX_EPISODE_STEPS=8) — next step env / sim method LLM / VLM gate / stop state rollout loop 1.1 Upstream invariants worth marking The judge is gated on completion. make_informed_decision only fires when the navigator self-reports Completion Estimation: Yes ; otherwise the agent silently stays on the current sub-instruction (no extra LLM call). base_il_trainer_llm.py:760, 841-843 STOP is decide-to-stop, not an env action. There is no STOP in the action space — the episode ends when the agent completes the last sub-instruction ( continue / stay at idx == len-1 while completion is Yes). decision_agent.py:957-959 12-direction binning is fixed. construct_image_dicts maps each predicted angle to one of 12 viewpoint ids (0 = current orientation, 30° apart). The navigator prompt's geometry depends on this exact mapping. base_il_trainer_llm.py:202-259 Per-sub-instruction history resets on continue . Advancing the pointer clears nav_history . base_il_trainer_llm.py:783-784 The judge sees a chosen-path image sequence seeded with the current view and appended each step. base_il_trainer_llm.py:487-491, 687 Default model is gpt-5 ( run.sh: --llm gpt-5-2025-08-07 ), shipped abilities are continue + stay only ( run_OpenNav.yaml: ENABLED_META_ABILITIES ), with no max_tokens cap. 2. The AgentCanvas port workspace/nodesets/method/threestep/__init__.py mirrors the upstream method bodies 1:1 within the framework floor. Because Three-Step makes ~4 LLM calls per step (navigator + two history-compression summaries + the gated judge) plus the pre-loop decompose, the floor is already 13 method nodes — most are single-responsibility prompt builders / parsers. The one fat node is judge_decide , the sole writer of all four state keys. The env ( env_habitat ), waypoint predictor ( opennav_waypoint ) and perception ( opennav_perception ) are reused unchanged from Open-Nav. 2.1 Per-step flow Step 1 — global (pre-loop) detect_actions_llm · extract_landmarks → iterIn init: action_list · landmark_list Step 2 — local (per step) panorama_rgbd · waypoint_predict bin_to_directions (12-dir) ram_tag · spatial_caption format_observation · navigator_prompt navigator_llm → parse_navigator completion · pred_vp judge_decide gated judge LLM (Yes) + decision rules + pointer/history machine + env action SOLE WRITER of all 4 state keys graph_state (episode) current_action_idx · nav_history chosen_images_b64 · move_stack stateContainer — lastWrite step_hightolow env_habitat (angle, distance) step.info iter_out — stop stop · final_stop stop final_stop evaluate → metrics graphOut iterOut → iterIn — next step (step_budget, default 8 mono / 20 calibration) env_habitat / FM threestep node llmCall loop control graph_state state access loop-back The loop is a v3 iterIn/iterOut scope: action_list / landmark_list ride the init_* ports (persist) and are re-emitted each step; select_subinstruction reads the pointer to emit the…

title: VoxPoser on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + voxposer port
text: VoxPoser on AgentCanvas — paper analysis + voxposer port VoxPoser (Huang et al. 2023; repo huangwl18/VoxPoser ) is a zero-shot manipulation method: a chain of Language Model Programs (LMPs) writes Python that composes 3-D value maps (affordance / avoidance / rotation / velocity / gripper) over a voxel grid, a greedy planner extracts a waypoint path, and the robot follows it. This page documents the decomposed, env-decoupled port — the 8-node local voxposer nodeset planning each subtask on a frozen GT scene snapshot and following waypoints closed-loop on LIBERO (Franka) via env_libero__step_pose in voxposer_libero_decomposed.json . Upstream's RLBench env is replaced wholesale; this is a cross-simulator port, not a reproduction on the paper's benchmark. At a glance Upstream VoxPoser (Huang et al. 2023) — repo huangwl18/VoxPoser pinned @ e3a4c9e (MIT). Core: src/interfaces.py (LMP_interface + execute() ), src/LMP.py , src/planners.py , src/controllers.py , 8 prompt files ( prompts/rlbench/ ), src/configs/rlbench_config.yaml . Env consumed env_libero — reset (metadata), observe_objects (privileged GT snapshot: BDDL object names, per-object point clouds, scene PC, EE pose, gripper, bounds), step_pose (8-vec or {pose, hold_steps} ; OSC follow with convergence), evaluate FM consumed None via canvas wires — the LMPs call the LLM through the vendored LMP._cached_api_call , rerouted to litellm with a disk cache ( ~/.cache/agentcanvas/voxposer_llm/ ). Graph pins gpt-4o · temp 0 · max_tokens 512 (upstream default: gpt-4). Method nodeset voxposer (8 nodes, local-mode in the backend process) + vendored upstream modules ( LMP.py , interfaces.py , planners.py , controllers.py , dynamics_models.py , utils.py , prompts/libero/ ) + port-added _runtime.py / _wired_env.py State Module-level _RUNTIMES[handle] cache (the per-episode 7-LMP runtime; handle issued by voxposer__init , rides wires as TEXT) + node-local cursor in dispense_waypoint ; no graph_state containers Graph workspace/graphs/vla/unverified/voxposer_libero_decomposed.json (17 nodes, 31 edges; outer subtask scope step_budget 8 + inner follow loop inside the waypoint_exec composite, step_budget 80) Fidelity faithful with justified deviations + 1 unexplained latent divergence (inventory-first audit 2026-06-11 against e3a4c9e ): 7/8 prompts and all planner/LMP configs byte-checked; plan/follow split fully recorded (commit a90cc7dc ); headline: object-centric (pushing) subtasks lose the MPC controller path (§3E) Status Pre-decoupling decomposed run: 3/5 success on libero_spatial task 7 (2026-06-03). GT-snapshot decoupling landed 2026-06-11 ( a90cc7dc ); post-decoupling smokes (1 ep, task 0) currently 0/1 — re-validation in progress (§4). 1. Upstream method analysis (Huang et al. 2023) Upstream runs everything in-process against a live RLBench env. Per instruction: Planner LMP ( prompts/rlbench/planner_prompt.txt ): the instruction becomes Python that calls composer("<subtask>") once per subtask, in order. LMP config: gpt-4 · temp 0 · max_tokens 512 · stop ["# Query: ", "objects = "] ( rlbench_config.yaml ). Composer LMP per subtask ( composer_prompt.txt , include_context: False ): writes code that calls parse_query_obj (entity grounding) and the five get_*_map LMPs, then execute(movable, affordance_map=…, …) . Value maps : each get_*_map LMP returns a callable evaluated lazily; unspecified maps fall back to current-state defaults ( interfaces.py ). Maps live on a map_size=100 voxel grid over the workspace bounds. Plan ( execute() first half, interfaces.py:169– ): object_centric = movable ∉ EE_ALIAS ( :183 ); the greedy voxel planner ( planners.py — gradient-free nearest-high-value descent with obstacle weighting, Savitzky–Golay smoothing, curvature cut) optimizes a path from the movable's position; postprocessing yields (position, rotation, velocity, gripper) waypoints. Follow ( execute() second half): per waypoint, controller.execute(movable_obs, waypoint) ( :162 ) — for the EE this reduces to a motion-planner move; for object-centric subtasks (pushing) it is a true MPC step : sample num_samples=10000 contact/push candidates through a heuristic PushingDynamicsModel , take the best, re-observe, repeat. After the loop, a final apply_action snaps to the last waypoint ( :184–205 , EE-centric only). Replan : max_plan_iter outer repetitions of plan→follow per subtask (config default 1 — effectively open-loop per subtask in sim). LLM caching : every API call goes through a pickle DiskCache keyed by the full request kwargs ( LLM_cache.py ) — re-runs are free. RLBench (live env) instruction + scene live object obs planner LMP → composer("…") ×K subtask composer LMP code per subtask parse_query_obj + 5 maps value-map callables greedy voxel planner planners.py · savgol execute(): plan half object_centric :183 maps path DiskCache LLM_cache.py · pickle by kwargs every LMP call cached on disk controller.execute MP move / MPC push PushingDynamics MPC 10000 samples · obj-centric one waypoint RLBench apply_action EE pose + gripper inner: for waypoint in path — re-observe movable each step subtasks exhausted composer calls done task success RLBench scoring outer: for subtask in plan — composer → maps → plan → follow simulator side planner / controller fn LMP (LLM-written code) loop guard disk cache data flow loops (inner / outer) 1.1 Upstream invariants worth marking The LLM writes code, the code runs. Every LMP exec() s generated Python in a namespace exposing detect / execute / get_*_map / numpy . The method's behaviour is the prompt files plus this namespace — nothing else. Planning is over value maps, not poses. The five map LMPs return callables ; the planner re-evaluates them lazily so maps always reflect the latest observation. object_centric selects the follow mechanism ( interfaces.py:183 ): EE subtasks get motion-planner moves; pushing subtasks get true MPC through PushingDynamicsModel . The planned path is the movable's path either way — for pushing, waypoints are object positions, not EE positions. max_plan_iter: 1 in the sim config — one plan per subtask, with num_waypoints_per_plan: 10000 commented "we only do open loop for sim". Replanning is a real-robot affordance. The disk cache makes runs reproducible — identical prompts at temp 0 replay from pickle, no API call. 2. The AgentCanvas port The port is a plan/follow split across a two-scope graph , with the planning stack fully env-decoupled (commit a90cc7dc , 2026-06-11). The vendored LMP stack runs local-mode in the backend process against a WiredEnv — a read-only env stand-in backed by a frozen GT snapshot from env_libero__observe_objects . This is sound because planning never steps the sim: every env read during one plan_subtask sees a single static state, so a snapshot is semantically identical to the live reads the retired in-subprocess adapter performed. Only two sim-driving entry points are stubbed in the composer's namespace: execute → LMP_interface.plan (plan, don't drive) and reset_to_default_pose → a single lift-to-home waypoint (z = 1.20). WiredEnv raises on any write call — the loud-failure guard motivated by the abandoned v2 design, which buffered execute() open-loop and scored SR 0. 2.1 Per-episode flow env_libero (pre-loop) reset → observe_objects GT snapshot (privileged) snapshot vp_init 7-LMP runtime + WiredEnv handle vp_planner composer capture-stub subtask list iter_in_outer init: handle·list·budget obs_loop fresh snapshot per iter vp_plan_subtask composer+maps+plan expand_for_settle 60-tick holds at flips 8-vec traj _RUNTIMES[handle] 7 LMPs + WiredEnv, backend process litellm + disk cache inside LMP waypoint_exec (composite — inner follow loop, step_budget 80) dispense_waypoint cursor over trajectory plan_executor → step_pose JSON env_libero step_pose OSC follow · hold_steps expanded trajectory inner: next waypoint until cursor exhausted or success vp_check_done success · all subtasks · budget episode_success · step_index iter_out_outer ·…

title: EQA Support Status
text: EQA Support Status Frank inventory — HM-EQA/OpenEQA envs, reasoner nodesets, gaps Related Capability: Graph-Expressible Agents · related design docs: Server Mode · Batch Eval This page does two jobs. Part I — Support answers which EQA methods AgentCanvas runs or wants to run : a single support matrix (verified · in-progress · planned), then the env / reasoner / method machinery underneath. Part II — Landscape places those methods in the wider EQA field — representative & most-cited reasoners and the 2024–26 frontier (§9) — condensed from the EQA — Methods survey. Companion to VLN Support Status and VLA Support Status . Part I — Methods we support 1. Support matrix Every EQA graph AgentCanvas ships , is building , or has planned (§8). This is the single source of truth for "what we support" — the env / reasoner / method backing is in §2–§4, planned adoptions in §8, the wider field is Part II. Verified graphs live under eqa/verified/ , the rest under eqa/unverified/ . Graph Status Headline result (HM3D, n=100, gpt-4o) openeqa_em_blind_llm.json ✅ verified working LLM-Match 0.4175 (paper baseline 0.355, +6 pp) — run_id=10864f2c openeqa_em_single_frame.json ✅ verified working LLM-Match 0.330 — single-arbitrary-frame ablation (paper has no entry for this archetype) — run_id=3aa974ba openeqa_em_multiframe.json ✅ verified working LLM-Match 0.7025 (paper baseline 0.466 GPT-4V K=50, +24 pp) — run_id=1f2e327b openeqa_aeqa_* (A-EQA) 🚧 TODO — blocked on data Active-EQA mode not yet shipped. Blocker is data , not method: the original OpenEQA Meta-Dropbox tarballs (full-fidelity scene frames + states re-render artefacts) are permanently expired (verified 2026-05-04, see scripts/data/fetch_dataset_openeqa.sh ); the HF mirror we use for EM-EQA caps each episode at 32 frames and doesn't carry the artefacts an active-explorer needs explore_eqa_hmeqa.json ✅ verified working Author-verified 2026-06-01 — runs end-to-end on a real HM-EQA episode (HM3D meshes installed, install_ac_hmeqa.sh done). Closes the E6 "validate end-to-end on a real episode" remaining item. tooleqa_hmeqa.json 🔁 redone 2026-06-08 (monolith) — runs e2e, SR tuning Rebuilt monolith-first : backend ReAct reasoner ( tooleqa__step ) + fused tooleqa_explore__go_next (full Explore-EQA TSDF frontier step + teleport — the first port's bug was treating go_next as discrete dead-reckoning). Qwen2.5-VL-3B serves the LLM/VQA; DetAny3D serves 2D/3D detect; all over server-mode HTTP. End-to-end smoke works (first correct answer: ep0 → 'B' == GT). SR aggregate + 3B convergence still being tuned — see roadmap #63. Scope still HM-EQA only; reuses the explore_eqa_hmeqa.json TSDF substrate. All three OpenEQA EM-EQA graphs reproduced on 2026-05-04. Frozen artefacts (summary + graph + log.jsonl.gz with raw VLM and judge text) live under outputs/archive/openeqa_em_baselines_2026-05-04/ — the README in that folder is the canonical reproduction record. Headline ordering matches paper expectation: multi-frame ≫ blind > single-arbitrary-frame. We outperform paper baselines because the reasoner + judge are gpt-4o (2025); the paper used gpt-4 / gpt-4-vision-preview (2024). The +24 pp gap on multi-frame is dominated by the model upgrade. VLN graphs are out of scope (see VLN Support Status ); VLA graphs are out of scope (see VLA Support Status ). Graph folder layout (2026-06-01). workspace/graphs/ is organised by domain × verification status. The EQA verified set is eqa/verified/{openeqa_em_multiframe, openeqa_em_single_frame, openeqa_em_blind_llm, explore_eqa_hmeqa}.json ; eqa/unverified/ is currently empty — its prior occupant tooleqa_hmeqa was removed 2026-06-08 pending a monolith-first redo (A-EQA graphs are also not yet shipped — data-blocked, see §2.3). Graphs stay addressable by bare name — the execution resolver and architect overlay fall back to a recursive graphs/**/{name}.json search. 2. Environment Nodesets EQA environments wrapped as BaseNodeSet s under server mode. Both share the ac-hmeqa conda env in server mode ( parallelism="replicated" ). 2.1 HM-EQA (E6, in progress) Files : workspace/nodesets/env/env_hmeqa/__init__.py + hmeqa_renderer.py + hmeqa_replay.py Source : House-Mesh EQA over HM3D — multi-choice questions on semantic scenes Capabilities : 5 tools incl. free-pose teleport Method side : paired with explore_eqa.py + explore_eqa_tsdf.py (see §3) Doc : HM-EQA nodeset Constraint discovered during integration (TODO #17): live TSDFPlanner objects don't cross the server-mode JSON IPC boundary (numba-JIT'd volumes aren't pickle-friendly). The explore-eqa nodeset works around this with subprocess-local module globals keyed by episode_id , not state containers — load-bearing design constraint for future container work 2.2 OpenEQA (E9, shipped 2026-05-04) File : workspace/nodesets/env/env_openeqa_em.py Source : OpenEQA EM-EQA (24.06) — free-form Embodied QA with LLM-as-judge (1–5 → LLM-Match) 5 tools : reset / episode_info / sample_frames / parse_score / emit_metrics — judge LLM call uses framework builtin llmCall ; OpenEQA-specific score parsing + metric naming live in two thin nodeset nodes Dataset path (the install actually works today): HuggingFace mirror Embodied1/open-eqa (32 frames/episode parquet) — installer rewritten to use this since the upstream Meta Dropbox tarballs are permanently expired ( scripts/data/fetch_dataset_openeqa.sh ) Critical config — image_size (default 512) : sample_frames long-side resize. Mirrors paper baseline ( openai_utils.py:30 ); the default keeps a 32-frame batch under provider single-request size caps (OpenAI rejects >50 MB; raw 1920×1080 PNGs total ~70 MB and silently fail otherwise — that bug was the reason early multi-frame smokes scored 1/5 across the board) Doc : OpenEQA nodeset 2.3 OpenEQA A-EQA (E10, deferred — blocked on data ) Active-EQA mode would reuse HM-EQA's Habitat manager + an explore-eqa-style termination policy on top of the E9 nodeset, plus a free-text answer-emit action. Not yet shipped — and the blocker is data, not method : The original OpenEQA Meta-Dropbox tarballs (full-fidelity HM3D / ScanNet frames + the states archive used for Habitat re-rendering) are permanently expired — verified 2026-05-04 ( Link Expired HTML response, see the rationale comment block at the top of scripts/data/fetch_dataset_openeqa.sh ). The HuggingFace mirror Embodied1/open-eqa is enough for EM-EQA (32 frames per episode, parquet) but does not carry the scene meshes / state artefacts an active explorer needs. Method-side dependencies (answer-emit verb on a unified action contract — TODO #46) are real but secondary; even if those landed today, A-EQA would still be blocked until we either (a) re-render via Habitat from a fresh path-fix patch + recovered states , or (b) find a community mirror of the original tarballs. 3. Reasoner / Method Nodesets EQA-shaped graphs are reasoner fragments , not full agents — pure (frames, question) → (answer, score) functions. They wrap as composite graph_nodes; default to no iter_in / iter_out (per project memory eqa_reasoner_shape ). 3.1 Explore-EQA (paper port) File : workspace/nodesets/explore_eqa.py Source : Explore-EQA — agent that explores HM3D scenes, scores VLM token-likelihoods over frontier choices, and decides when to answer Pairs with : HM-EQA env nodeset (§2.1) — example graph explore_eqa_hmeqa.json Backend lock-in (v1) : Prismatic-locked. ExploreEQANodeSet.initialize() loads Prismatic; vlm.get_loss() is called directly from VLMScoreStep.forward() — same pattern as policy_cma . Decoupling tracked as TODO #50 Doc : ExploreEQA nodeset 3.2 Explore-EQA TSDF utilities File : workspace/nodesets/explore_eqa_tsdf.py Source : vendored TSDFPlanner + geom utilities from explore-eqa, kept verbatim with minor tweaks Why a separate file : TSDFPlanner state is what hits the pickle/JSON-IPC wall mentioned in §2.1 — keeping it in its own module makes the subprocess-local-globals pattern easier to reason about 3.3 OpenEQA reasoner nodes File : same…

title: Support Status
text: Support Status Frank per-domain inventories of what AgentCanvas can actually do — envs covered, methods reproduced, headline numbers, current gaps. Snapshots, not architecture. These are registries, not design docs — they change when nodeset coverage changes, not when the architecture does. That is why they live next to the nodeset catalog rather than under design-docs/ (moved 2026-06-11). VLN Support Status Frank inventory — VLN envs, tools, methods, graphs, gaps VLA Support Status Frank inventory — VLA envs (LIBERO/SIMPLER), policy_vla, gaps EQA Support Status Frank inventory — HM-EQA/OpenEQA envs, reasoner nodesets, gaps

title: VLA Support Status
text: VLA Support Status Frank inventory — VLA envs (LIBERO/SIMPLER), policy_vla, gaps Related Capability: Graph-Expressible Agents · related design docs: Server Mode · Batch Eval This page does two jobs. Part I — Support answers which VLA methods AgentCanvas runs or wants to run : a single support matrix (verified · in-progress · retired), then the env / policy / method machinery underneath. Part II — Landscape places those methods in the wider VLA field — Track-A trained policies, Track-B zero-shot manipulation, and the 2024–26 frontier (§8) — condensed from the VLA — Methods survey. Companion to VLN Support Status and EQA Support Status . Part I — Methods we support 1. Support matrix Every VLA graph AgentCanvas ships or is building (retired graphs kept for the record). This is the single source of truth for "what we support" — the env / policy / method backing is in §2–§4, the wider field is Part II. Verified graphs live under vla/verified/ , the rest under vla/unverified/ . Graph Status Notes vla_policy_libero.json ⏳ unverified — gym-interface migration (2026-06-10) Rewired for the gym-like env contract (reset metadata-only, step_continuous , pull obs via observe_egocentric ); moved to vla/unverified/ pending re-eval. Runtime-blocked by TODO #66 — the policy_vla Pi0 graph path has never run on the vendored tree (the 2026-05-02 verification used scripts/smoke/smoke_vla_libero.py against external vlaworkspace); 3 of 4 debt layers fixed 2026-06-11. Pre-migration baseline (2026-05-02): Pi0 LIBERO finetune step=30000 , libero_spatial / task 0 , 5/5 success, avg 83.8 steps, 5.4 s wall vla_policy_simpler.json ❓ status TBD — please confirm Graph + nodeset shipped under E11 (2026-05-01); no author-verified smoke result recorded in roadmap yet octo_simpler.json ✅ baseline runs SIMPLER eval uses native baselines (RT-1-X / Octo, not Pi0 finetunes); checkpoints under data/vla_policy/checkpoints/ . Mirror upstream's no-close pattern between episodes (SAPIEN 2.2.2 GC ordering bug — see §6.4) voxposer_libero_monolithic.json 🗑 deleted (2026-06-10) Removed with the VoxPoser↔LIBERO decoupling: its engine interleaved LMP exec with sim stepping inside one node, which cannot cross a wire boundary. Last verified state: author-verified 2026-06-01, full grasp + transport. The decomposed graph is the sole VoxPoser path now. voxposer_libero_decomposed.json ✅ verified (libero_object) — 2026-06-30 Planning moved to the backend-local voxposer nodeset, fed by the privileged GT snapshot from env_libero__observe_objects ; follow loop drives env_libero__step_pose closed-loop. Verified on libero_object : 44/50 = 0.88 at 50-ep suite scale (merged runs 20260628_202814 / 204422 / 211004 ) after fixing 3 stacked convention bugs (per-scene z-bounds anchored to robot0_base ; gripper action +1 =close; top-down grasp geometry). Scope caveat : full-VAS aggregate is 58/199 = 0.29 — only the object suite is fixed; libero_spatial (0.18) / libero_goal (0.10) / libero_10 (0.00) remain grasp-geometry-limited. Promoted to vla/verified/ 2026-06-30. This page is fresh — the verified rows have hard ground-truth datapoints from the author. Update the others as runs are confirmed. Graph folder layout (2026-06-30). workspace/graphs/ is organised by domain × verification status. The VLA verified set is vla/verified/{voxposer_libero_decomposed}.json (object-suite, promoted 2026-06-30); vla/unverified/ holds octo_simpler , vla_policy_libero , and vla_policy_simpler . (Note: octo_simpler shows "✅ baseline runs" in §1 but physically lives in vla/unverified/ — its verified-set membership is unconfirmed pending a recorded SIMPLER datapoint.) Graphs stay addressable by bare name — the execution resolver and architect overlay fall back to a recursive graphs/**/{name}.json search. VLN graphs are out of scope (see VLN Support Status ); EQA graphs are out of scope (see EQA Support Status ). 2. Environment Nodesets Manipulation simulators wrapped as BaseNodeSet s under server mode. Both ship in folder form — <name>/__init__.py plus sidecar wrappers — so the binary-incompatible upstream code stays packaged with its owner. 2.1 LIBERO (E12) Folder : workspace/nodesets/env/env_libero/ ( __init__.py + _wrapper.py ) Source : LIBERO manipulation benchmark — robosuite + MuJoCo, 7-D action contract Coverage : 130 tasks across 5 suites — libero_spatial / object / goal / 10 / 90 Why it's in : every modern VLA paper reports it; "fact-on-the-ground 2024–25 VLA benchmark" Bounds calibration (2026-05-16, superseded 2026-06-28) : z ∈ [+0.85, +1.25] is the libero_spatial (table-mounted) frame only — NOT canonical across suites. Floor-mounted suites ( libero_object , robot base z≈0) sit ~0.9 m lower; baking that single z box as a constant left every libero_object grasp target unreachable → 0/50 for 29 AAS iterations (misread as an OSC step-budget problem). Fixed 2026-06-28 : derive the z box per scene from robot0_base z ( _Z_BELOW_BASE=0.10 / _Z_ABOVE_BASE=0.35 ), x/y fixed. (The earlier DEFAULT_BOUNDS = z∈[-0.1, +0.6] "EE freezes at z=0.94" symptom — 30 cm below the table — was a separate, older red herring.) Doc : (no per-nodeset page yet — TBD) 2.2 SIMPLER (E11) Folder : workspace/nodesets/env/env_simpler/ ( __init__.py + _wrapper.py ) Source : SimplerEnv real-to-sim VLA eval — SAPIEN + ManiSkill2_real2sim Coverage : 25 tasks — 4 WidowX/Bridge + 21 Google Robot Why it's in : drop-in eval of any HF-hosted VLA checkpoint (low friction, cross-paper adoption) Doc : SIMPLER nodeset 3. Policy Adapter Nodeset A single registry hosts all VLA policies behind a uniform 4-stage pipeline. 3.1 VLA Policy (E13) Folder : workspace/nodesets/policy/policy_vla/ adapters/ — env↔canonical and canonical↔model adapters (per env × per model matrix) models/ — model-specific I/O shape conversions policies/ — checkpoint loaders + predict() per policy family presets/ — graph snippets for common (env, policy, ckpt) combos Independent conda env : ac-vla-policy Decomposition — 4 explicit canvas nodes per VLA call, replacing vlaworkspace's monolithic Adaptor system: adapt_env_to_canonical → adapt_canonical_to_model → predict → adapt_model_to_env Policy registry (4 today): Pi0 , SmolVLA , Diffusion Policy (DP) , DROID-DP Checkpoints : Lightning .ckpt and safetensors both supported by Pi0Policy.load_checkpoint Doc : Policy VLA nodeset This is a generic VLA inference framework, not a Pi0/SmolVLA/DP wrapper. Adding a new policy = adding a policies/<name>.py + adapter rows; the 4-node decomposition is reused unchanged. 3.2 VoxPoser-LIBERO ( workspace/nodesets/method/voxposer/ ) Motion-planning method (LMP + dynamics models). Not a policy in the policy_vla sense — instead an LMP-style planner that builds a voxel cost map and emits an OSC waypoint trajectory. Env-decoupled as of 2026-06-10 : the whole planning stack (7 LMPs, voxel maps, path planner) runs local-mode in the agentcanvas backend ; its only env coupling is the privileged GT snapshot wire ( env_libero__observe_objects ) in and waypoint actions ( env_libero__step_pose ) out — any env exposing that observe/step pair can host VoxPoser. Pairs with : voxposer_libero_decomposed.json (✅ verified on libero_object 2026-06-30, SR 0.88 @50ep; other suites still open). The monolithic graph was deleted 2026-06-10 — its LMP-exec↔sim-stepping interleave cannot cross a wire boundary Why the snapshot is sound : plan_subtask never steps the sim, so all env reads during one plan see a single static state — a frozen snapshot is exactly equivalent to the retired in-subprocess live-adapter reads. The follow loop still drives every waypoint closed-loop (the 2026-05 v2 WiredEnv attempt failed (SR=0) because it buffered whole execute() trajectories open-loop; the backend WiredEnv now raises on any write call) GT is explicit : VoxPoser's ground-truth perception is now a first-class privileged observation space on the env, visible in graph topology, instead of a hidden in-subprocess adapter Bounds : see §2.1 — the calibrated…

title: VLN Support Status
text: VLN Support Status Two purposes — what we support, and where it sits in the field This page does two jobs. Part I — Support answers which VLN methods AgentCanvas runs or wants to run : a single support matrix (shipped · in-progress · planned · wanted), then the env / tool / method machinery underneath. Part II — Landscape places those methods in the wider VLN literature — the representative and most-cited methods, and the 2025–2026 frontier — so the supported subset is legible against the whole field. Full per-era method narrative lives in the research survey VLN — Methods ; this page is the status + map view. Part I — Methods we support 1. Support matrix Every VLN method AgentCanvas ships , is building , or wants to build . This is the single source of truth for "what we support" — the env / tool / method backing is in §2–§4, the wider field is Part II. ⭐ marks the PortBench v1 / AAS-seed set. Method Env Status Graph / roadmap MapGPT-MP3D ⭐ MP3D discrete ✅ verified mapgpt_mp3d.json · §4.2 SmartWay-CE Habitat-CE ✅ verified smartway_ce.json · §4.6 SmartWay-mono-CE Habitat-CE ✅ᵖ paper-comparable (SR 0.270 vs 0.29) smartway_mono_ce.json · §4.6 DiscussNav-MP3D ⭐ MP3D discrete ✓ in-progress discussnav_mp3d.json · §4.7 · M3 Three-Step Nav Habitat-CE ✓ in-progress (faithful; SR 0/10 gpt-5-mini, needs gpt-5-full) threestep_ce.json · §4.9 NavGPT-MP3D ⭐ MP3D discrete ✅ verified (gpt-4 only) navgpt_mp3d.json · §4.1 NavGPT-CE Habitat-CE ⏳ unverified navgpt_ce.json · §4.1 · M1 Open-Nav Habitat-CE ⏳ unverified opennav_habitat.json · §4.3 SpatialNav-MP3D MP3D discrete ⏳ unverified (platform-internal) spatialnav_mp3d.json · §4.4 CMA / Seq2Seq (Straightforward) Habitat-CE ⚠️ legacy straightforward.json · §4.5 AO-Planner ⭐ Habitat-CE ✓ in-progress (SR 0.10 / oracle 0.20 @20ep, noisy) aoplanner_ce.json · §4.8 · M7 InstructNav ⭐ Habitat-CE 🅼 planned M8 — value-map argmax CA-Nav Habitat-CE 🅼 planned M9 — constraint-aware (v2) AgentVLN Habitat-CE 🅼 planned M10 — VLM-as-Brain 3B (v2) MapGPT (metric-grid) Habitat-CE 🅼 planned M2 — depth-derived map variant VLN-SIG MP3D discrete 🅼 planned M4 — sub-instruction grounding HAMT MP3D discrete 🅼 planned (neural policy) M5 DUET MP3D discrete 🅼 planned (neural policy) M6 Today's load-bearing demo set is the ✅ rows — mapgpt_mp3d , smartway_ce , smartway_mono_ce (+ navgpt_mp3d on gpt-4). The ✓ in-progress rows run end-to-end but aren't paper-comparable yet; the ⏳ unverified rows are not a code-quality verdict — most are prompt / tool-wiring / config issues or model-tier gaps (gpt-5-mini vs gpt-5-full) not yet chased down. Verified graphs live under workspace/graphs/vln/verified/ , the rest under vln/unverified/ (bare-stem resolution still works via WorkspaceComponentRegistry.resolve_graph_path ). 2. Environment nodesets VLN simulators wrapped as server-mode BaseNodeSet s (own subprocess + Python env). 2.1 Habitat (VLN-CE) File : workspace/nodesets/env/env_habitat.py ; pinned to the ac-vlnce conda env (Python 3.8) by the habitat-sim 0.1.7 wheel. Datasets : R2R-CE today; RxR-CE planned (E2). Env panel : HabitatEnvPanel — set_split / set_episode / peek_episode (ADR-server-002). Action space : Discrete(4) — STOP / FORWARD / LEFT / RIGHT. VRAM : actual peak in 20-worker replicated runs ~22.6 GB / 24 GB; the scheduler's 8000 MB declaration is ~3× conservative. 2.2 Matterport3D (MP3D) File : workspace/nodesets/env/env_mp3d/__init__.py — MatterSim discrete panoramic navigation. Datasets : R2R · R4R · RxR · REVERIE · CVDN / NDH — all six schemas behind one nodeset via the dataset → split → episode_index cascade in MP3DEnvPanel . Action space : dynamic-cardinality discrete viewpoints ( viewpoint_id TEXT + cand_vpids_json ) — unified with Habitat's once TODO #46 lands. 3. Perception & tool nodesets Method-side nodesets that expose tools any VLN agent can wire — they don't pick a method themselves. Nodeset File Provides Basic Agent nodesets/common/basic_agent.py Foundational toolkit — 11 nodes (action parsing, history, prompt assembly, …) NavGPT MP3D Tools nodesets/method/navgpt_mp3d_tools.py Online BLIP-2 captioning + Faster R-CNN detection Open-Nav perception nodesets/method/opennav_perception.py RAM (Swin-L 14M tags) + SpatialBot-3B (Phi-3 VLM, depth-aware captioning) Open-Nav waypoint nodesets/method/opennav_waypoint/ Frozen BinaryDistPredictor_TRM + ResNet50 RGB + DDPPO depth → 120×12 angle/distance with NMS SAM nodesets/model/model_sam.py Segment Anything — spatial layout & object localisation (feeds AO-Planner M7) Grounding DINO nodesets/model/model_grounding_dino.py Open-vocabulary detection — AO-Planner affordance grounding (reuses the detany3d env) InstructBLIP nodesets/model/model_instructblip.py Instruction-tuned captioning — DiscussNav scene description SSG nodesets/method/ssg.py Spatial Scene Graph — top-down render, reset, instruction-aware object query Geometry nodesets/common/geometry.py MeasureDistance and small utilities 4. Method nodesets Reasoning-side nodesets implementing a specific VLN paper / family. (Status per §1.) 4.1 NavGPT — nodesets/method/navgpt.py LLM-as-reasoning-core: action parsing, history management, output formatting. Pairs with navgpt_mp3d.json (discrete) and navgpt_ce.json (CE). NavGPT-MP3D is verified with gpt-4 — only gpt-4 yields a non-zero SR; gpt-4o over-confidently STOPs on long ReAct prompts and collapses to SR=0. NavGPT-CE remains SR=0. 4.2 MapGPT — nodesets/method/mapgpt.py ✅ ACL 2024 — linguistic topological map inside the LLM prompt + adaptive multi-step planning. First VLN method validated end-to-end on AgentCanvas. Pairs with mapgpt_mp3d.json . Use the MapGPT_72 subset (216 eps) for paper-comparable headlines; paper SR 0.477 (gpt-4v) / 0.463 (gpt-4o). 4.3 Open-Nav — nodesets/method/opennav.py ICRA 2025 — prompts, parsers, ensemble / fusion / tie-break, history; wired to opennav_perception + opennav_waypoint on Habitat-CE. Pairs with opennav_habitat.json (currently SR=0). 4.4 SpatialNav — nodesets/method/spatialnav.py MP3D discrete — 8-compass observation + SSG enrichment fork of NavGPT-MP3D. Platform-internal extension, not a paper port. ("Pre-exploration via SLAM" is in fact MP3D ground-truth .house + _semantic.ply , not SLAM.) Pairs with spatialnav_mp3d.json . 4.5 Policy CMA — nodesets/policy/policy_cma.py Cross-Modal Attention VLN-CE baseline as a single canvas node; batched ( batched=True , batch_dim="raw_obs" ) so K eval workers share one forward pass. Hidden state on explicit hidden_in / hidden_out ports. Pairs with straightforward.json (legacy). 4.6 SmartWay / SmartWay-mono — nodesets/method/smartway/ + smartway_mono/ ✅ VLN-CE waypoint-predictor family. smartway dual-frame, smartway_mono single-frame. smartway-mono SR 0.270 vs paper 0.29 after two 2026-05-16 fixes ( place_id + image_labels wiring); smartway-CE author-verified 2026-06-01. Default planner LLM gpt-5-mini (needs temperature=1.0 + max_tokens=2000 ). Pairs with smartway_ce.json , smartway_mono_ce.json . 4.7 DiscussNav — nodesets/method/discussnav.py ✓ MP3D multi-agent discussion — 8-expert LLM fan-out → aggregator. Pairs with discussnav_mp3d.json ; fitness not yet driven to a paper-comparable number. Exercises F3 parallel execution. 4.8 AO-Planner — nodesets/method/aoplanner/ ✓ AAAI 2025 — Affordances-Oriented Planning: two-VLM Visual Affordances Prompting (VAP) over SAM ground masks + projected waypoints, with a SmartWay-cloned action decider. Faithful pure-foundation-model port — the released code's supervised ETPNav TRM waypoint predictor + ZeroShotGraphMap alignment are intentionally dropped. Uses model_grounding_dino for affordance grounding. Pairs with aoplanner_ce.json ; runs end-to-end — headline SR 0.10 / oracle 0.20 @20ep (noisy: gpt-5-mini is forced to temp=1.0, so run-to-run SR variance at N≤20 exceeds any tuning delta; the multi-hop controller has hit SPL 0.899 on individual episodes; paper-comparable headline needs gpt-5-full + the 216-ep MapGPT72 slice) ( M7 ). See the AO-Planner nodeset page. 4.9 Three-Step…

title: Demo Plan — Showcase vs Tutorial
text: Demo Plan — Showcase vs Tutorial Proposal for the demo portfolio the project should build. Demos fall into two categories with very different design constraints — showcase (grab attention in 10–30 seconds) and tutorial (walk a researcher through a full loop in 5–30 minutes). This page is the living plan; each demo gets promoted into tutorials/ or a standalone landing asset once it is produced. 1. Design Tradeoff Axis Showcase Tutorial Audience Hasn't read the README — reviewer, potential collaborator, X scroller Has decided to try it — reads carefully Length 10–30 s visible payoff 5–30 min end-to-end Voice Visual impact, minimal text Step-by-step, every step has a "why" Win condition Stars / shares / lead capture Completion rate + reproducibility Kiss of death Front-loaded setup Hand-waving, skipped steps The project's nine capabilities (see capabilities/ ) are the pool of ideas to hit: ① graph-expressible agents · ② customizable node system · ③ isolated runtime envs · ④ nested graphs · ⑤ graph executor · ⑥ real-time observability · ⑦ hook system · ⑧ batch evaluation & job queue · ⑨ visual canvas editor. Each demo should land on at least one of these explicitly. 2. Showcase Demos Four demos, each targeted at one distribution surface (hero GIF, tweet video, paper teaser, architecture poster). 2.1 Drag three nodes, watch an agent navigate Capability target: §1 Graph-Expressible Agents + §6 Real-Time Observability Empty canvas. Drag env_habitat__observe + llmCall + env_habitat__step + iterIn / iterOut out of the sidebar. Connect. Press Play. The right panel renders live Habitat RGB while the agent drives. One unbroken screen-recording, ~20 seconds. Why this one first : it's the cleanest expression of one JSON = one agent = one graph . No code, no config files, no batch harness — just a canvas. Distribution surface : hero GIF on index.md + README top. 2.2 Live state + multi-panel observability Capability target: §6 Observability + state container system (ADR-dataflow-002, ADR-dataflow-004) Run a MapGPT-style graph where a StateContainer holds the accumulated topo-map. Four viewer sinks on screen at once: RGB, depth, topo-map rendered from the container, textScroll of the LLM's thought stream. All four update in lock-step on every iterOut boundary. Why this one : a poster-worthy still frame and a hypnotic 60-second video. Most competing agent frameworks show a log tail or a single image; four synchronized panels drives home that observability is a first-class concern. Distribution surface : landing-page tweet + arXiv companion page header. 2.3 Swap one node, different agent Capability target: §2 Customizable Node System + decoupling Same graph; swap llmCall from GPT-4o to Claude Sonnet (just change the model on the node's inline config) — re-run. Then swap the policy node from policy_cma__forward to policy_hamt__forward — re-run. No wires touched. Show a small bar chart of SPL on val_unseen across the three variants. Why this one : reviewers love comparisons, and the framing "change one block, rerun, compare" is exactly the argument for the framework. Doubles as a mini ablation. Distribution surface : paper teaser figure + blog post cover image. 2.4 Save graph as node, drop into another graph Capability target: §4 Nested Graph System (ADR-dataflow-001, ADR-canvas-003) Open a working NavGPT graph. Select all → "Save as Graph Node" → name it NavGPTSubagent . Open a larger "high-level planner" graph. The NavGPTSubagent appears in the Explorer sidebar. Drag it onto the canvas — it shows up as a single block with the PortIn/PortOut ports exposed. Wire it up. Run. Same behaviour. Why this one : the nested graph system is the most architecturally interesting feature and the hardest to convey in a still image. A live drag-and-drop sells it in 15 seconds. Distribution surface : "advanced architecture" callout on the landing page; reused inside F12 AAS tutorial. 3. Tutorial Demos Four tutorials organized "from user to author", each aligned with one existing capability doc and ending in a working artifact the reader can commit back. 3.1 Your first agent in 5 minutes Capability target: §1 Graph-Expressible Agents + §6 Observability Install via scripts/install/ + run_dev.sh Open workspace/graphs/straightforward.json Edit the prompt in promptTemplate → change one phrase Select val_unseen split + episode 0 in the EnvContextBar Press Play; watch SPL in the metrics sink at the end Takeaway : the reader has run a real VLN-CE agent without writing code. 3.2 Build a custom node in Python Capability target: §2 Customizable Node System (ADR-components-004 Python-driven UI) Create workspace/nodes/word_count.py with a WordCountNode(BaseCanvasNode) subclass Declare input_ports = [PortDef("text", "TEXT")] , output_ports = [PortDef("count", "METRICS")] Add a ui_config = NodeUIConfig(color="amber", config_fields=[...]) Hot-reload via POST /api/components/reload Drag the node onto a canvas, wire it, run Takeaway : one Python class = one visible canvas node. No frontend code touched. 3.3 Wrap a simulator in server mode Capability target: §3 Isolated Runtime Environments (ADR-server-001) Build a minimal MiniSimNodeSet(BaseNodeSet) with two tools ( reset , step ) in workspace/nodesets/env/env_mini_sim.py Set server_python = "/path/to/other/env/python" Load via the NodeSet Manager page with mode=server Observe: framework spawns a subprocess, fetches /manifest , generates proxy canvas nodes automatically Confirm the proxy nodes are indistinguishable from local nodes Takeaway : any tool that needs a different Python env can still appear on the canvas. Zero server-side code written by hand. 3.4 Run batch eval with the worker pool Capability target: ADR-eval-002 Worker-Pool Batch Eval Open the Eval page Pick the graph from §3.1; pick val_unseen split; set worker_count=4 Launch; watch four per-worker lanes render in parallel Export the resulting EvalRun JSON Takeaway : the same canvas graph runs at 4× throughput under batched evaluation — no graph-side changes, tagged env subprocesses handled invisibly. 4. Capability Coverage Cross-check — every capability is covered by at least one demo. Capability Showcase Tutorial §1 Graph-Expressible Agents 2.1 3.1 §2 Customizable node system 2.3 3.2 §3 Isolated runtime envs — 3.3 §4 Nested graph system 2.4 (future — Tutorial 5) §5 Dataflow executor 2.1, 2.2 (implicit) 3.1 §6 Real-time observability 2.2 3.1 §7 Hook system — (future — Tutorial 6) Gaps: hook system has no dedicated demo yet; nested graphs only have showcase coverage. Candidates for a second pass once the first nine ship. 5. Roadmap Alignment The tutorial demos directly serve three open TODO items: TODO #22 Install instructions — 3.1 forces us to finish this. TODO #41 Per-category contributor guide — 3.2 and 3.3 become the per-category pilots. TODO #48 Validate /implement-graph pipeline end-to-end — 3.2 + 3.3 in sequence is exactly this exercise. The showcase demos piggy-back on existing graphs ( straightforward.json , NavGPT-CE from TODO #6) — they do not block on new features. 6. Production Order Slot Demo Rationale 1 Showcase 2.1 + Tutorial 3.1 (paired) Share the same straightforward.json ; one screen-recording session produces both assets 2 Showcase 2.2 Highest "wow per second" for social distribution; uses the existing MapGPT graph 3 Tutorial 3.2 Fastest tutorial to produce; also unblocks TODO #41 4 Showcase 2.3 Needs a second LLM profile + HAMT (M5) — gate by whichever lands first 5 Tutorial 3.3 Requires writing a toy sim; medium effort 6 Showcase 2.4 Needs a polished NavGPT graph (TODO #6) first 7 Tutorial 3.4 Hangs off the existing Eval page; smallest new content The 1 + 3 pairing is the best ROI: one recording run, two published assets. Changelog 2026-04-21 11:03: Initial content — four showcase demos, four tutorial demos, capability coverage matrix, roadmap alignment, production order.

title: My Solo Development Procedures
text: My Solo Development Procedures 1. Docs Maintenance 1.1 Core Docs The project must maintain these 6 docs in docs-site/docs/ : Doc Purpose Update when blueprint.md Problem → Solution → Principles → Components → Features Goals or direction shift architecture.md System diagram, data flow, tech stack Structure changes glossary.md Domain terms and definitions New concepts introduced decisions/ ADRs — key choices and rationale, per-field Any non-trivial technical decision roadmap.md Done / In Progress / Planned / Deferred Features ship or priorities change my-procedures.md This file — how I work Process evolves 1.2 Feature Docs Feature design docs in docs-site/docs/design-docs/ : Doc Purpose Update when canvas-system.md Canvas system — composite nodes, nesting, navigation, node specs Canvas architecture changes graph-system.md Graph data model — GraphDefinition, file management, API, serialization Graph model or API changes graph-executor.md Dataflow execution engine — node firing, iteration, state Executor behavior changes wire-types.md Wire type system — type catalog, serialization, canvas colors Wire types added or changed nodesets.md NodeSet system — local vs server, lifecycle, examples NodeSet API changes plugin-servers.md Plugin server architecture — BaseServer, ServerApp, manifest protocol Server system changes state-containers.md Dual-wire state system — visible containers, reducers, checkpointing State system or container types change (add more as features grow) 1.3 Implementation Docs Technical reference docs in docs-site/docs/concepts/ , tutorial/ , recipes/ , api-reference/ : Doc Purpose Update when graph-expressible-agents.md Agent forms — DAG, agent loop, mixed composition Agent form or IterIn/IterOut changes customizable-node-system.md Node system — base classes, ports, auto-discovery Node system API changes isolated-runtime-environments.md Runtime isolation — server mode, AutoServerApp, proxy nodes Runtime or isolation changes nested-graph-system.md Nested graphs — subgraph, PortIn/PortOut, flatten Graph nesting changes graph-execution-engine.md Dataflow executor — firing rules, state containers Executor behavior changes real-time-observability.md Observability — WebSocket, output viewers, streaming Observability system changes 1.4 Doc-Site — Single Source of Truth Live documentation site ( docs-site/ , MkDocs Material) hosts all project docs Serve locally: bash docs-site/run_dev.sh (live reload on save) 1.5 Change Tracking Every doc has a last updated: timestamp and a ## Changelog section at the bottom When a decision ripples across docs: write the ADR first ( /adr ), then propagate to each affected doc Each affected doc gets its own changelog entry referencing the ADR (e.g. "see ADR-platform-001") Changelog 2026-04-19 18:11: style compliance retrofit — added ToC + Changelog section

title: CI / CD
text: CI / CD The repo's CI/CD in one line: a GitHub Actions workflow continuously deploys the doc-site to GitHub Pages. There is no build/test CI yet — those checks run locally via pre-commit. AgentCanvas's CI/CD is a single GitHub Actions workflow, .github/workflows/docs.yml — continuous deployment of the doc-site to GitHub Pages. There is no continuous integration (build/test) yet; code-quality gates (lint, format, secret scan, commit format) run locally via pre-commit , so keep running them before you push. on this page Deploy docs workflow · What it implies §1 The "Deploy docs" workflow It runs on a push to master that touches docs/** (or the workflow file itself), and can also be triggered manually ( workflow_dispatch ). It uploads the docs/ tree as the Pages artifact and deploys it — no build step , because the hand-authored docs/ tree is the site. A concurrency group ensures only one deploy runs at a time. name : Deploy docs on : push : branches : [ master ] paths : - ' docs/** ' - ' .github/workflows/docs.yml ' workflow_dispatch : permissions : contents : read pages : write id-token : write # Avoid in-flight overlap; only one deploy at a time. concurrency : group : pages cancel-in-progress : false jobs : deploy : runs-on : ubuntu-latest environment : name : github-pages url : ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }} steps : - uses : actions/checkout@v4 - uses : actions/configure-pages@v5 - name : Upload Pages artifact uses : actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with : # The docs/ tree IS the built site — no build step needed. path : ./docs - name : Deploy to GitHub Pages id : deployment uses : actions/deploy-pages@v4 §2 What it implies The whole docs/ tree is published. The artifact is path: ./docs — every file under docs/ ships to Pages as-is. Large binaries that don't need publishing should therefore live outside docs/ (e.g. the README hero GIFs are kept at the repo-root assets/readme/ , not under docs/ ). No build, no markdown step. Pages serves the hand-authored HTML directly; the only "build" is the local _wrap_handwritten.py chrome pass that you run before committing (see html-authoring ). Triggered by docs changes only. A push that doesn't touch docs/** (or the workflow) does not redeploy. Use workflow_dispatch to force a deploy. No test/lint CI yet. Quality gates are local-only via pre-commit . Adding backend/frontend test CI is a future item.

title: Contributing to AgentCanvas
text: Contributing to AgentCanvas Two kinds of contribution, both welcome: grow the content ecosystem, or improve the core. AgentCanvas is a visual agent-design platform for embodied AI research — you prototype agents by drawing node graphs that run in real time against simulators (Habitat-Sim, MatterSim, SAPIEN/ManiSkill2, MuJoCo/robosuite) or real hardware. There are two ways to contribute: content — write a nodeset or compose a graph out of nodesets — and core — improve the framework itself (UI, backend, features, refactors). For version numbering, tagging, and release flow, see the Versioning Policy . on this page Content · Core · Dev loop & checks · PR format · Getting help §1 Content — nodesets & graphs (most contributions) This is where most research lands: you extend what AgentCanvas can do , without touching the framework. Write a nodeset — a self-contained capability others can drop into a graph. Two flavors: Tool nodesets turn a capability into nodes — e.g. a real-time 3D Gaussian-Splatting reconstructor, a voxel-based SLAM system, an object detector, or a new simulator. Method nodesets encode an agent's reasoning — e.g. a NavGPT nodeset, a MapGPT nodeset. Or compose a graph — a JSON that wires existing nodesets into a complete agent method. Often no new Python: the contribution is the topology that turns a pile of nodes into a strong graph agent. How: open a PR adding your files under workspace/ ( nodesets/ , nodes/ , or graphs/ ). Review is light — it loads, follows the component conventions , and stays inside workspace/ . Start from workspace/nodesets/example.py for a nodeset, or an existing file under workspace/graphs/ for a graph; the nodeset docs catalog what already exists. You keep the credit. Every nodeset and graph is attributed to its author/maintainer on the Credits board — maintained in the README and a dedicated doc-site page — so contributing here, rather than in a separate repo, doesn't cost you authorship. If your contribution has an associated paper, its citation link goes on the Credits board too. §2 Core — UI, backend, framework (also welcome) Suggestions, new features, and even refactors of the core ( agentcanvas/backend/app/ , agentcanvas/frontend/ , docs/ ) are all welcome — the core isn't closed. One ask: if a change is big enough to cost real time, open a GitHub Discussion first and talk it through before you build. A few minutes aligning up front beats sinking days into something that turns out not to fit. Small fixes and self-contained features can go straight to a PR. Two hard constraints any core PR must respect: Import boundary: agentcanvas/backend/app/ never imports domain libraries ( habitat , habitat_sim , vlnce_baselines , habitat_baselines ) — those live in workspace/ (enforced by agentcanvas/backend/app/test_import_boundary.py ). Contract changes need an ADR: touching BaseCanvasNode , BaseNodeSet , PortDef , NodeUIConfig , ConfigField , DisplayField , or wire types affects every nodeset author, so it goes through an ADR . §3 Dev loop & checks For the full install flow (conda envs, submodules, data), read the Installation guide . Once installed, the minimal dev loop is: # Activate the agentcanvas env (Python 3.10+, ADR-platform-004) conda activate agentcanvas # Start the canvas (backend :8000 + frontend :5173) cd agentcanvas && bash run_dev.sh # Doc-site on :8092 (live reload via SSE) bash docs/run_dev.sh Install pre-commit once after clone, then run the full check set before you push: pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install pre-commit run --all-files — whitespace, EOF newline, YAML/JSON validity, file-size limit Backend + import boundary: cd agentcanvas/backend && python -m pytest Backend smoke: cd agentcanvas && bash run_dev.sh — server starts and /docs loads Frontend: cd agentcanvas/frontend && npm run build (or npm run typecheck ) Docs: no build step — open the page in bash docs/run_dev.sh (:8092) and confirm it renders §4 PR format Branch from main ( feat|fix|refactor|docs/<short-desc> ), and keep one concern per PR — the full branch → PR → merge flow is on the Git Workflow page. Commits follow Conventional Commits: type(scope): description , lowercase, imperative, ≤72 chars ( feat , fix , refactor , docs , chore , test , ci , perf ). The style rules live in .claude/standard/ — read the one for your task before writing code. ## Summary - What changed and why (1–3 bullets) ## Reference Closes #N, or links the Discussion this was agreed in ## Test plan - [ ] How you verified it works (pytest / npm run build output) §5 Getting help Roadmap: Roadmap Talk before a big change: GitHub Discussions Who built what (+ citations): Credits (doc-site) · README What nodesets already exist: Nodeset docs Glossary: glossary — read before touching canvas / graph / state features Design rationale: Architecture Decisions — ADRs grouped by field Repository layout: Repo guide Issues: github.com/jianzhou0420/AgentCanvas/issues

title: Git Conventions
text: Git Conventions How commits, branches, and pull requests are named in AgentCanvas — the commit format is machine-enforced by pre-commit. AgentCanvas follows Conventional Commits . The commit-message format is enforced at the commit-msg stage by the conventional-pre-commit hook ; branch and PR conventions are by agreement. The canonical source is .claude/standard/git-conventions.md . For the end-to-end branch → PR → merge flow, see Git Workflow . on this page Commit messages · Branch naming · Pull requests §1 Commit messages Format: type(scope): description . Type (required) — one of: feat , fix , refactor , docs , chore , test , ci , perf . Scope (optional) — one of: backend , frontend , eval , orchestrator , data , infra . Description — lowercase, imperative mood, max 72 characters. feat(backend): add state container system fix(frontend): correct edge rendering on zoom docs(infra): update architecture diagram refactor(backend): simplify graph executor loop Enforcement The conventional-pre-commit hook validates the subject line at commit time, but only after you enable the commit-msg stage: pre-commit install --hook-type commit-msg (see Pre-commit Hooks § Install ). The accepted types are exactly the list above. §2 Branch naming Branch from main ; name the branch after the change type: feat/short-description fix/short-description refactor/short-description docs/short-description §3 Pull requests PRs cover all contributions — a nodeset or graph under workspace/ gets a light review; a core change is welcome too (open a Discussion first if it's big). Keep one concern per PR, ensure the local checks pass, and follow the PR description template. The full process — what gets reviewed, required checks, and the description format — is in Contributing § PR format . PR titles follow the same type(scope): description convention as commits.

title: Git Workflow
text: Git Workflow Every change lands on main through a pull request — one review flow, two entry points that converge the moment the PR is open. Changes to AgentCanvas reach the main branch through pull requests , not direct pushes. It is a single flow with two entry points: maintainers branch inside the repository, external contributors branch on a fork. From the moment the PR is opened the two paths are identical — automated checks run, a reviewer reads the diff, and the change is squash-merged into main . The only difference is the one extra fork step an external contributor takes at the very start. on this page Two entry points · Step by step · What lands on main · Related Two entry points — a maintainer branch in the repo and an external fork — both open a pull request that goes through CI and review before a squash-merge into main. Maintainer branch in the repo External contributor fork → branch Pull Request → main CI + Review checks + approve main One flow, two entry points. The fork is the only step external contributors add; everything from “open a PR” onward is the same for everyone. §1 Two entry points, one flow Whether you can push a branch to the repository directly or need a fork depends only on your access level. After the pull request is opened, the process is identical. Maintainer / collaborator (write access) External contributor (no write access) Start Clone the repository directly. Fork to your account, then clone the fork. Branch Create a feat/… branch in the repo. Create a feat/… branch on your fork. Open PR Branch → main , same repo. Fork branch → upstream main (cross-repo PR). After the PR Identical — CI runs, a reviewer reads the diff, then it is squash-merged into main . So it is not two processes but one: the external path just adds a fork as its entry point. This mirrors how the framework is meant to be extended — you contribute into this repo (nodesets, graphs, or core), and you keep authorship on the Credits board. §2 Step by step Sync main . git checkout main && git pull . Branch by change type. git checkout -b feat/<short-desc> — one of feat|fix|refactor|docs , per Git Conventions § Branch naming . Commit in focused chunks. Conventional Commits ( type(scope): description ), and keep one concern per PR — see Git Conventions § Commit messages . Run the checks locally before pushing. pre-commit run --all-files , the backend pytest , and the frontend build — the full list is in Contributing § Dev loop & checks . Push your branch. git push -u origin feat/<short-desc> (to the repo if you have write access, otherwise to your fork). Open the PR against main . e.g. gh pr create --base main . Fill the description template (summary / reference / test plan) from Contributing § PR format ; the PR title uses the same type(scope): description convention. Address review. Push follow-up commits to the same branch — CI re-runs and the PR updates in place. Merge. On approval the PR is squash-merged into main and the branch is deleted. §3 What lands on main main is the single integration branch, and it is kept releasable: PR-only. Changes land through a pull request; please don't push directly to main . Checks must pass. Pre-commit hooks and CI gate every PR — see Pre-commit Hooks and CI . Reviewed before merge. A reviewer reads the diff; discussion happens on the PR. Talk first for big changes. If a change is large enough to cost real time, open a GitHub Discussion before you build — small, self-contained changes can go straight to a PR. Squash-merge PRs merge as a single squashed commit, so main 's history reads one meaningful change per merge rather than every intermediate work-in-progress commit on the branch. §4 Related Git Conventions — commit, branch, and PR-title naming (the format is machine-enforced by pre-commit). Contributing — what gets reviewed, the dev loop & local checks, and the PR description template. CI · Pre-commit Hooks — the automated checks that gate a PR. Versioning Policy — how releases are numbered, tagged, and cut once a change is on main .

title: Repository Governance
text: Repository Governance The meta-documents that govern the AgentCanvas repository — how it is versioned, contributed to, licensed, and checked at commit time. This section gathers the repository's governance documents — how AgentCanvas is versioned, contributed to, licensed, and checked at commit time. The version number is driven by API stability and is kept independent of paper milestones; that decoupling, plus security and citation guidance, are spelled out in the Versioning Policy . §1 Governance documents Document What it covers Status Versioning Policy SemVer numbering, tags, releases, release candidates, and the citation axis Active Contributing Two kinds of contribution (content: nodesets & graphs · core: framework), dev loop, PR format, repo layout Active License Apache-2.0 summary and license compatibility Active Git Conventions Commit-message format (Conventional Commits), branch naming, PR title rules Active Pre-commit Hooks The commit-time check suite (hygiene, ruff, prettier, gitleaks, conventional commits) and how to install it Active CI / CD The GitHub Actions workflow that deploys the doc-site to GitHub Pages Active Auto-Memory Store How Claude Code's auto-memory is stored in-repo: symlinked store, topic routing, and the per-directory git split Active §2 The governance model in one line A small, tightly-held core (evolved through ADRs ) plus a large content ecosystem — nodesets and graphs contributed by PR into workspace/ and credited on the Credits board. Most contributions are content (light review); the framework itself is contributed via reviewed PRs, with a Discussion first for anything big. The full breakdown is in Contributing ; the paradigm-level meaning of each major version is in Major Versions .

title: License
text: License AgentCanvas is released under the Apache License 2.0.
